zh.lifeSummary: 當HENRY LE CHILDE於1190年出生在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利時，他的父親WILLIAM LE CHILDE28歲，母親Lady William le Childe29歲。他於大約1216年與Anabel Bedfordshire在英國武斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1258年在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当HENRY LE CHILDE于1190年出生在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利时，他的父亲WILLIAM LE CHILDE28岁，母亲Lady William le Childe29岁。他于大约1216年与Anabel Bedfordshire在英国武斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1258年在英国武斯特斯爾伍斯特享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: LE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: CHILDE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Child
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: HENRY
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When HENRY LE CHILDE was born in 1190, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, his father, WILLIAM LE CHILDE, was 28 and his mother, Lady William le Childe, was 29. He married Anabel Bedfordshire about 1216, in Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1258, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque HENRY LE CHILDE est né en 1190, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, WILLIAM LE CHILDE, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Lady William le Childe, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Anabel Bedfordshire environ 1216, à Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1258, à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als HENRY LE CHILDE 1190 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater WILLIAM LE CHILDE 28 und seine Mutter Lady William le Childe 29. Er hat um 1216 in Worcestershire, England Anabel Bedfordshire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1258 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Worcester, Worcestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando HENRY LE CHILDE è nato il nel 1190 nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, WILLIAM LE CHILDE, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Lady William le Childe, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Anabel Bedfordshire circa 1216, nel Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1258, nel Worcester, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: HENRY LE CHILDEが1190年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーで生まれた時，父WILLIAM LE CHILDEは28歳，母Lady William le Childeは29歳であった。約1216年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアでAnabel Bedfordshireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1258年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: HENRY LE CHILDE(이)가 1190년 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 WILLIAM LE CHILDE(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady William le Childe(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1216년 영국 워스스터실르에서 Anabel Bedfordshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1258년 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando HENRY LE CHILDE nasceu em 1190, em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, WILLIAM LE CHILDE, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Lady William le Childe, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Anabel Bedfordshire aproximadamente 1216, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1258, em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда HENRY LE CHILDE родился в 1190 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени WILLIAM LE CHILDE было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady William le Childe, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Anabel Bedfordshire около 1216 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1258 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando HENRY LE CHILDE nació en 1190, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, WILLIAM LE CHILDE, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Lady William le Childe, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Anabel Bedfordshire aproximadamente 1216, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1258, en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 68 años.
